body_txt: These bad guys have the skills and abilities to make tons of money, seize incredible power, and achieve amazing (evil) things... but they are stuck in a petty crook mentality. These villains are very much the opposite of the Visionary Villain . Despite having the means to get everything they want, they don't think to apply their talents intelligently and instead try to do things normal criminals do. It's not that they aren't ruthless enough or fear the consequences of being ambitious and failing, and all they do is Poke the Poodle rather than Kick the Dog , or that if someone Cut Lex Luthor a Check they'd be more successful in legitimate business. The Small-Minded Villain can be ruthless, their skills may be well geared towards evil, and may enjoy being a Card-Carrying Villain too much to ever give up being Just a Gangster . They just don't think big enough. A hero, hostage, and even other villains will be Disappointed by the Motive upon finding out that they have no higher goals beyond petty crime, or the imagination to try better schemes. Calling them out , or asking " And Then What? " will confuse or anger the small-minded villain, but occasionally they may reply with this being all they know how to do, or that this is precisely what they want to do. Their lack of bigger goals means other villains tend to hire them into their grander plots. A Dynamic villain may even learn that they're misusing their talents, and not in the " You Could Have Used Your Powers for Good! " way. They'll probably smarten up and start making more thought-out Evil Plans . A character arc for such a villain could be to achieve Team Rocket Wins and Take Over the City . For the record, this trope isn't about complaining about "dumb" villains; this trope can be legitimately used to characterize villains. While it's often used for comedy , it can also serve as a dramatic or tragic characterization for a villain who has great potential but squanders it. If this trope is taken to the Logical Extreme , you may even end up with a Harmless Villain , especially in children's media. Evil counterpart to Small Steps Hero . See also Sliding Scale of Villain Threat , where this villain may be an able combatant but their scope for evildoing is small. See also Comically Small Demand . Compare Reed Richards Is Useless , wherein a super could use their abilities to solve all the world's problems, but their ability is solely used to resolve their specific plot points instead. See also/compare Mundane Utility , which is using amazing powers for mundane things. Also compare Minion with an F in Evil , for minions who just can't bring themselves to do fiendish acts, no matter how hard they try. Also compare Sanity Has Advantages and Do Wrong, Right . Contrast Evil Is Petty , where a villain commits major acts of evil, but still commits minor ones too just because they can. While this villain exclusively commits minor acts of evil, despite having the capability for worse. Also contrast Humble Goal , where it's not that the villain themselves has a modest ambition and rejects a bigger one, but that they've not thought of a bigger one or resist doing so. Also contrast Brilliant, but Lazy , where a villain might only do small crimes to get enough to get by despite being brilliant.
